headline: Political Philosophy: Justice, Liberty, and the Social Contract
description: Political philosophy addresses fundamental questions of governance: What is justice? What justifies state authority? The social contract tradition — Hobbes through Locke and Rousseau to Rawls — provides the dominant Western framework.

citation.name: Justice: What's the Right Thing to Do? (2025 Updated Edition)
citation.author: Sandel M.J.

citation.name: Political Philosophy in the Twenty-First Century (2025)
citation.author: multiple
